GS3.headline: Government Launches 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana' for Farmer Welfare
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana', a new comprehensive scheme aimed at enhancing farmer welfare and agricultural productivity.
• The scheme integrates direct income support, subsidized credit, crop insurance, and access to modern farming technologies under a single umbrella.
• It seeks to build upon the success of existing schemes like PM-KISAN and PM Fasal Bima Yojana, providing a more holistic support system for farmers.
• Special emphasis will be placed on small and marginal farmers, women farmers, and farmers in drought-prone regions.
• The initiative is expected to boost agricultural income, reduce rural distress, and contribute to India's food security goals.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India—scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: The scheme is named 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana'.
It integrates direct income support, credit, and insurance.
It builds on schemes like PM-KISAN and PM Fasal Bima Yojana.
Special focus is on small, marginal, and women farmers.
Aims to enhance farmer welfare and agricultural productivity.
mainsPoints: **Objectives:** Increase farmers' income, reduce input costs, mitigate risks (weather, market price), promote sustainable farming practices, ensure food security, reduce rural-urban migration.

**Components:** Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) for income support, easy access to institutional credit at low interest rates, comprehensive crop insurance coverage, promotion of farm mechanization, digital platforms for market access and information.
**Challenges:** Ensuring equitable access, preventing leakages, effective implementation in diverse agro-climatic zones, addressing land fragmentation, linking farmers to value chains.
**Impact:** Improved living standards for farmers, reduced farmer suicides, enhanced agricultural output, diversification of crops, rural economic upliftment.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer smiling in a field, symbolizing prosperity.
GS3.trivia: The PM-KISAN scheme (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi) provides income support of ₹6,000 per year to eligible farmer families.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework Unveiled for Private Sector Participation in Space Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today announced a comprehensive new policy framework aimed at significantly increasing private sector participation in India's space activities.
• The policy delineates roles for ISRO as a facilitator and regulator, while opening up opportunities for private players in satellite manufacturing, launch services, and space-based applications.
• It includes provisions for financial incentives, technology transfer, and access to ISRO's facilities and expertise for eligible private entities.
• The move is expected to foster innovation, create jobs, attract investment, and make India a global hub for commercial space services.
• This policy aligns with the broader vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and leverages India's growing capabilities in space technology.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The policy aims to increase private sector participation in space.
ISRO will act as a facilitator and regulator.
Incentives include financial support, tech transfer, and facility access.
The policy aligns with 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
Private players can engage in satellite manufacturing, launch, and applications.
mainsPoints: **Benefits of Private Participation:** Boosts innovation, reduces ISRO's burden, creates a robust space ecosystem, attracts FDI, generates high-skill jobs, makes India competitive in global space market, faster deployment of space applications.

**Challenges:** Regulatory clarity, funding for startups, ensuring level playing field, intellectual property rights, data security, competition with established global players, capacity building for private workforce.
**Government's Role:** Creating a predictable regulatory environment (IN-SPACe), providing initial handholding, ensuring fair access to resources, promoting R&D, acting as an anchor customer.
**Global Context:** India is following a global trend of commercialization of space, learning from models like NASA's collaboration with SpaceX.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a rocket launching with gears, symbolizing private industry involvement.
GS3.trivia: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre) was established in 2020 to provide a level playing field for private companies.

GS3.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Ethics
GS3.explainer:
• India today commenced hosting a major International Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ethics, bringing together global experts, policymakers, and industry leaders.
• The conference aims to develop a shared understanding and common principles for responsible AI development and deployment, focusing on fairness, accountability, transparency, and safety.
• Discussions included mitigating biases in AI algorithms, ensuring data privacy, addressing job displacement concerns, and regulating autonomous weapons systems.
• India highlighted its commitment to human-centric AI and the importance of inclusive AI governance frameworks that respect diverse societal values.
• The outcomes are expected to contribute to global dialogues on AI regulation and potentially influence national AI strategies.
gsSubTopics: Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Role of science and technology in the development of India.
prelimsPoints: India hosted the International Conference on AI Ethics.
Key principles discussed include fairness, accountability, transparency, and safety (FATS).
Topics covered included algorithmic bias, data privacy, and autonomous weapons.
India emphasized human-centric AI.
The conference aims to contribute to global AI regulation discussions.
mainsPoints: **Ethical Concerns in AI:** Bias and discrimination (e.g., in hiring, policing), privacy violations, lack of transparency ('black box' problem), accountability for AI errors, potential for misuse (deepfakes, surveillance), impact on employment, autonomous decision-making.

**Importance of AI Governance:** Ensures AI development aligns with societal values, mitigates risks, builds public trust, promotes equitable access to AI benefits, prevents ethical dilemmas from escalating.

**India's Approach:** Focus on 'AI for All' and 'Responsible AI', leveraging AI for social good (healthcare, agriculture), developing indigenous AI capabilities, participating in global forums to shape AI norms.

**Way Forward:** Multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society; developing national AI strategies with ethical guidelines; investing in AI research for safety and robustness; international cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized brain image with interconnected nodes, representing AI and ethical considerations.
GS3.trivia: India launched its National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in 2018, emphasizing 'AI for All'.

GS3.headline: Major Strides in Indigenous Development of 5G Advanced Technology
GS3.explainer:
• India today announced significant progress in the indigenous development of 5G Advanced technology, positioning itself as a key player in next-generation telecom.
• Researchers from IITs and leading telecom firms showcased advancements in 5G-Advanced standards, focusing on enhanced mobile broadband, ultra-low latency, and massive machine-type communications.
• This development is crucial for India's digital transformation, enabling new applications in smart cities, IoT, industrial automation, and immersive experiences.
• The 'Made in India' push for 5G Advanced aims to reduce reliance on foreign technology, bolster national security, and create export opportunities.
• Government support through funding and policy initiatives has been instrumental in accelerating this indigenous innovation drive.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India is making strides in indigenous 5G Advanced technology.
It focuses on enhanced mobile broadband, ultra-low latency, and massive machine-type communications.
Development involves IITs and leading telecom firms.
Aims to reduce reliance on foreign technology and create export opportunities.
Government support is crucial for this initiative.
mainsPoints: **Economic Impact:** Boosts digital economy, creates new industries and jobs, facilitates innovation in various sectors, strengthens India's position in global telecom supply chain, potential for technology exports.
**Strategic Importance:** Enhances national security by reducing dependence on foreign vendors, enables secure communication infrastructure, supports critical infrastructure development.

**Applications:** Transforms sectors like healthcare (telemedicine, remote surgery), education (VR/AR learning), agriculture (precision farming), manufacturing (Industry 4.0), smart cities (traffic management, utilities).
**Challenges:** High investment costs, spectrum availability, cybersecurity threats, ensuring widespread and equitable access, training skilled workforce, global standardization issues.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'National Logistics Policy 2025' Aiming for Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved the 'National Logistics Policy 2025', a comprehensive framework designed to reduce logistics costs and enhance India's global trade competitiveness.
• The policy aims to streamline multi-modal transportation, integrate digital systems, and develop a skilled workforce for the logistics sector.
• Key objectives include bringing down logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to below 8% by 2030, a benchmark comparable to developed economies.
• It focuses on creating a single window system for logistics services, promoting green logistics, and leveraging technology for efficiency and transparency.
• This strategic policy is expected to boost manufacturing, exports, and overall economic growth by improving supply chain efficiency.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The policy is named 'National Logistics Policy 2025'.
It aims to reduce logistics costs to below 8% of GDP by 2030.
Focuses on multi-modal transportation and digital integration.
Promotes green logistics and a single window system.
Approved by the Union Cabinet.
mainsPoints: **Importance of Logistics:** Crucial for economic growth, trade, manufacturing, and e-commerce; reduces costs, improves efficiency, enhances competitiveness, connects producers to markets.
**Challenges in Indian Logistics:** High costs, fragmented infrastructure, lack of digital integration, skill gaps, regulatory complexities, environmental impact.

**Policy Objectives:** Improve Logistics Performance Index (LPI) ranking, create a unified regulatory framework, promote technology adoption (AI, IoT, blockchain), develop logistics parks, enhance skill development.
**Impact:** Boosts 'Make in India' initiative, facilitates ease of doing business, attracts investment, creates jobs, reduces wastage, improves last-mile delivery, strengthens global supply chains.
GS3.imageDescription: Container ships, trucks, and trains symbolizing multi-modal logistics.
GS3.trivia: India's Logistics Performance Index (LPI) ranking improved significantly in recent years, but still lags behind global leaders.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Mission for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare today launched the 'National Mission for Climate-Resilient Agriculture' (NMCRA).
• The mission aims to develop and promote climate-resilient farming practices, technologies, and infrastructure to mitigate the impact of climate change on Indian agriculture.
• Key focus areas include drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, soil health improvement, and climate-smart livestock management.
• The initiative will integrate research, extension services, and farmer training to build adaptive capacity across vulnerable regions.
• NMCRA is a crucial step towards ensuring food security and sustainable agricultural growth in the face of increasing climate variability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: The mission is named 'National Mission for Climate-Resilient Agriculture' (NMCRA).
Launched by the Union Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare.
Aims to develop climate-resilient farming practices and technologies.
Focuses on drought-resistant crops, water management, and soil health.
Integrates research, extension services, and farmer training.
mainsPoints: **Necessity:** Indian agriculture is highly vulnerable to climate change (erratic monsoons, droughts, floods, extreme temperatures); impacts food security, farmer livelihoods, and rural economy.

**Components:** Development of new crop varieties (heat-tolerant, drought-resistant), precision agriculture, improved irrigation techniques (micro-irrigation), agroforestry, crop diversification, early warning systems, farmer education.
**Challenges:** Scale of implementation, financial resources, farmer adoption of new practices, land fragmentation, lack of climate data at local levels, market linkages for diversified crops.

**Benefits:** Enhanced food and nutritional security, stable farmer incomes, reduced environmental footprint of agriculture, improved soil health, biodiversity conservation, contribution to climate change adaptation and mitigation.
GS3.imageDescription: A lush green field with diverse crops, symbolizing resilient agriculture.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top 10 most climate-vulnerable countries globally, making agricultural resilience critical.
